---
title: "Teaching"
---

## University of Glasgow

**Data for Development** (MSc [Global Development](https://www.gla.ac.uk/postgraduate/taught/global-development/)). Whis course addresses a skills gap identified through consultations with aid practitioners: quantitative literacy among development practitioners. The course bridges statistical methods with policy applications, making data analysis accessible to students without strong quantitative backgrounds.

**[Political Economy of Conflict](https://www.gla.ac.uk/coursecatalogue/course/?code=POLITIC5101)** (MSc). Examines how international economic forces shape armed conflict, state-building, and political violence.

**[International Political Economy](https://www.gla.ac.uk/coursecatalogue/course/?code=POLITIC4160)** (Honours). Theoretical foundations and contemporary debates in IPE.

## Methods training

I also teach quantitative methods at international summer programmes in the social sciences.

[ICPSR Summer Program](https://www.icpsr.umich.edu/web/pages/sumprog/), University of Michigan — Linear Regression, 2019–2021, 2023–2026

[Odum Institute](https://odum.unc.edu/), University of North Carolina — Linear Regression, 2024–2025

[Essex Summer School](https://essexsummerschool.com/) in Social Science Data Analysis — Generalized Linear Models, 2017–2019

## Supervision

I supervise doctoral students working on political economy, conflict, and development. See my [CV](../cv/index.html) for current and completed supervisions, or [get in touch](mailto:patrick.shea@glasgow.ac.uk) to discuss potential projects.
